mission.title: harbor: [Feature Request] Provide a UI to visualize container vulnerabilities
mission.description: [Feature Request] Provide a UI to visualize container vulnerabilities. This issue affects 34+ users.

steps.title: Identify harbor troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=harbor
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: [Feature Request] Provide a UI to visualize container vulnerabilities

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
When trying to identify which containers within our registry contain vulnerabilities, it is frustrating to have to check every individual tag, within every individual registry, within every individual

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature Request] Provide a UI to visualize container vulner
steps.description:
Thank you for contributing to Harbor!

# Comprehensive Summary of your change
This PR aims to enable the Vulnerability Dashboard feature in Harbor 

# Issue being fixed

Community Proposal: [Link](https://github.com/goharbor/community/pull/222)

Added the decision on the finalized approach to add th
```yaml
curl -Ss --header "authorization: Basic $AUTH" -X GET "$HARBOR_SERVER/api/v2.0/projects/$PROJECT_NAME/repositories/$REPO_NAME/artifacts" | jq -r '.[].addition_links.vulnerabilities.href'
```

steps.title: Confirm [Feature Request] Provide a UI to visualize contai is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Thank you for contributing to Harbor!

# Comprehensive Summary of your change
This PR aims to enable the Vulnerability Dashboard feature in Harbor 

# Issue being fixed

Community Proposal: [Link](https://github.com/goharbor/community/pull/222)

Added the decision on the finalized approach to add the feature is discussed in the community proposal: https://github.com/goharbor/community/pull/222. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
